description: NCERT Class 10 (Intro + Heights & Distances), Class 11 (Trigonometric Functions), Class 12 (Inverse Trig), and JEE Mains/Advanced + IIT-level olympiad problems.  Covers function evaluation, identity proofs, equation solving, height-distance applications, and law of sines/cosines.  Most right-triangle and height-distance problems include a labelled SVG figure.

questions.question_text: Evaluate the inverse trigonometric expression using the principal value branch (NCERT Class 12 Ch 2).
(IIT-JEE) Each sin θᵢ is at most 1, so equality forces each to equal 1.  Determine the only possible θᵢ in [0, 2π].
questions.answer_plain: sintheta_i = 1 forall i Rightarrow theta_i = pi/2; ;; theta_1 + theta_2 + theta_3 = frac{3pi}{2}.

questions.question_text: A helicopter flying horizontally at height 500 m observes two milestones on the ground (in the same vertical plane below it). The angles of depression are 30° and 75° respectively. Find the horizontal distance between the two milestones.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 25 and one leg of length 7. Find the other leg.

questions.expression_latex: A = 40^\circ, \; a = 8, \; b = 13; \quad \text{(SSA — solve the triangle)}
questions.question_text: (Ambiguous SSA case)  Given angle A = 40°, side a = 8, and side b = 13, determine how many triangles are possible and solve each one. Use the law of sines: sin B = b · sin A / a, then check whether the supplement also yields a valid triangle.
Prove using the triple-angle formula cos(3x) = 4 cos³ x − 3 cos x.
questions.answer_latex: \cos(3x) = 4\cos^3 x - 3\cos x \Rightarrow 4\cos^3 x = \cos(3x) + 3\cos x; \;\; \cos^3 x = \frac{3 \cos x + \cos(3x)}{4}.
questions.answer_plain: cos(3x) = 4cos^3 x - 3cos x Rightarrow 4cos^3 x = cos(3x) + 3cos x; ;; cos^3 x = frac{3 cos x + cos(3x)}{4}.

questions.expression_latex: \theta = 0; \quad \text{find the point on the unit circle: } (\cos\theta, \sin\theta)
questions.question_text: Identify the point on the unit circle corresponding to θ = 0.  State the coordinates (cos θ, sin θ) in exact form.
(IIT-JEE classic) Use the product-of-cosines formula with N angles in arithmetic progression.
questions.answer_latex: \text{Multiply both sides by } 2\sin(\pi/7); \text{ telescoping gives } \frac{\sin(8\pi/7)}{2^3 \sin(\pi/7)} = -\frac{\sin(\pi/7)}{8 \sin(\pi/7)} = -\frac{1}{8}; \text{ note sign convention picks up } \cos(3\pi/7) > 0.
questions.answer_plain: text{Multiply both sides by } 2sin(pi/7); text{ telescoping gives } frac{sin(8pi/7)}{2^3 sin(pi/7)} = -frac{sin(pi/7)}{8 sin(pi/7)} = -frac{1}{8}; text{ note sign convention picks up } cos(3pi/7) > 0.

questions.question_text: Find the maximum and minimum values of f(x) = 8 sin x + 6 cos x.  Use the identity a sin x + b cos x = √(a² + b²) sin(x + φ).
State the identity and prove it by setting θ = sin⁻¹(x) and applying complementary-angle reasoning.
Express in terms of an angle in [0°, 45°] using the complementary-angle identity:  sin(90° − θ) = cos θ,  cos(90° − θ) = sin θ, tan(90° − θ) = cot θ, cot(90° − θ) = tan θ.

questions.expression_latex: \text{In any triangle ABC, prove: } \sin A + \sin B + \sin C \leq \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}.
questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE) Use Jensen's inequality on the concave function sin x on (0, π), with x = A, B, C summing to π.
questions.answer_latex: \sin x \text{ is concave on } (0, \pi); \;\; \frac{\sin A + \sin B + \sin C}{3} \leq \sin\!\frac{A+B+C}{3} = \sin\!\frac{\pi}{3} = \frac{\sqrt 3}{2}; \;\; \sin A + \sin B + \sin C \leq \frac{3\sqrt 3}{2}; \text{ equality at equilateral triangle.}
questions.answer_plain: sin x text{ is concave on } (0, pi); ;; frac{sin A + sin B + sin C}{3} leq sin!frac{A+B+C}{3} = sin!frac{pi}{3} = frac{sqrt 3}{2}; ;; sin A + sin B + sin C leq frac{3sqrt 3}{2}; text{ equality at equilateral triangle.}

questions.question_text: Express in terms of an angle in [0°, 45°] using the complementary-angle identity:  sin(90° − θ) = cos θ,  cos(90° − θ) = sin θ, tan(90° − θ) = cot θ, cot(90° − θ) = tan θ.

State the principal-value identity for sin⁻¹(sin θ).
questions.answer_latex: \sin^{-1}(\sin\theta) = \theta, \;\; \theta \in [-\pi/2, \pi/2]; \; \text{otherwise need to reduce to the principal range.}
questions.answer_plain: sin^{-1}(sintheta) = theta, ;; theta in [-pi/2, pi/2]; ; text{otherwise need to reduce to the principal range.}

questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE) Use the identity cos A + cos B + cos C = 1 + r/R where r, R are inradius and circumradius, with r ≤ R/2 (Euler).
questions.answer_plain: 1 + frac{r}{R} leq 1 + frac{1}{2} = frac{3}{2}; text{ equality at equilateral.}

Express in terms of sin x, cos x, or tan x.  Use tan(3x) = (3 tan x − tan³ x) / (1 − 3 tan² x).
questions.answer_plain: frac{3 tan x - tan^3 x}{1 - 3 tan^2 x}

questions.answer_latex: \sin(3x) = 3\sin x - 4\sin^3 x \Rightarrow 4\sin^3 x = 3\sin x - \sin(3x); \;\; \sin^3 x = \frac{3 \sin x - \sin(3x)}{4}.
questions.answer_plain: sin(3x) = 3sin x - 4sin^3 x Rightarrow 4sin^3 x = 3sin x - sin(3x); ;; sin^3 x = frac{3 sin x - sin(3x)}{4}.

questions.answer_plain: frac{1 - cos x}{sin x} = frac{2 sin^2(x/2)}{2 sin(x/2) cos(x/2)} = frac{sin(x/2)}{cos(x/2)} = tan(x/2).

questions.expression_latex: \tan A + \tan B + \tan C = \tan A \tan B \tan C, \quad A + B + C = \pi
questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE classic) Prove the identity for the angles of any triangle.
questions.answer_latex: A + B = \pi - C \Rightarrow \tan(A + B) = -\tan C \Rightarrow \frac{\tan A + \tan B}{1 - \tan A \tan B} = -\tan C; \;\; \tan A + \tan B = -\tan C + \tan A \tan B \tan C; \;\; \tan A + \tan B + \tan C = \tan A \tan B \tan C.
questions.answer_plain: A + B = pi - C Rightarrow tan(A + B) = -tan C Rightarrow frac{tan A + tan B}{1 - tan A tan B} = -tan C; ;; tan A + tan B = -tan C + tan A tan B tan C; ;; tan A + tan B + tan C = tan A tan B tan C.

questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE) Combine using sum-to-product; the equation factors with a common term.
questions.answer_latex: \text{Both sides factor with } (1 + 2\cos x); \; \sin 2x = \cos 2x \Rightarrow \tan 2x = 1; \;\; x = \pi/8, 5\pi/8 \text{ in } (0, \pi).

questions.expression_latex: \sin A + \sin B + \sin C = 4 \cos(A/2) \cos(B/2) \cos(C/2), \quad A + B + C = \pi
questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE classic) Prove this triangle-sum identity using sum-to-product twice.
questions.answer_latex: \sin A + \sin B = 2 \sin\!\frac{A+B}{2}\cos\!\frac{A-B}{2}; \sin C = \sin(A+B) = 2 \sin\!\frac{A+B}{2}\cos\!\frac{A+B}{2}; \text{ combine and use } \frac{A+B}{2} = \frac{\pi-C}{2}.
questions.answer_plain: sin A + sin B = 2 sin!frac{A+B}{2}cos!frac{A-B}{2}; sin C = sin(A+B) = 2 sin!frac{A+B}{2}cos!frac{A+B}{2}; text{ combine and use } frac{A+B}{2} = frac{pi-C}{2}.

questions.answer_latex: \sin^2 x + \cos^2 x = 1 \Rightarrow \frac{\sin^2 x}{\cos^2 x} + 1 = \frac{1}{\cos^2 x}; \;\; \tan^2 x + 1 = \sec^2 x.
questions.answer_plain: sin^2 x + cos^2 x = 1 Rightarrow frac{sin^2 x}{cos^2 x} + 1 = frac{1}{cos^2 x}; ;; tan^2 x + 1 = sec^2 x.

questions.answer_latex: \tan(x + y) = \tan(\pi/2 - z) = \cot z; \;\; \frac{\tan x + \tan y}{1 - \tan x \tan y} = \frac{1}{\tan z}; \text{ cross-multiply and rearrange.}

questions.answer_plain: tan A tan B tan C geq left(frac{tan A + tan B + tan C}{3}right)^3; text{ combined with Sigma = product, get } Sigma geq 3sqrt 3.

questions.question_text: In triangle ABC, sides a and b are 10 and 4 units, with included angle C = 150°. Find the area using the formula  Area = (1/2) · a · b · sin C.
(AAS) Two angles and one side are given.  Find the third angle and the remaining two sides.  Use the law of sines a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C.
